description_long: This object consists of several slender, green stems supporting delicate, pale purple flowers with multiple thin petals. The flowers are arranged at varying heights, with some blooms fully open and others partially closed. The petals are narrow, slightly pointed, and radiate outward from a central disk, giving the flowers a starburst appearance. The leaves are lanceolate, medium green, and have serrated edges, attached alternately along the stems. The overall structure is airy and lightweight, with the flowers positioned at the ends of the stems. This type of flower is commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or roadside plantings, adding a touch of natural beauty to the environment.
description: A cluster of slender green stems with pale purple, starburst-shaped flowers and serrated lanceolate leaves. The flowers are delicate and arranged at the ends of the stems.
description_view_0: Two main stems with pale purple flowers and several lanceolate leaves are visible, with one flower facing forward and another angled to the side.
description_view_1: The tallest flower is prominent, with overlapping petals and more leaves visible along the stems, showing a denser arrangement.
description_view_2: Side view highlights the thinness of the stems and the profile of the flowers, with petals radiating outward and leaves extending laterally.
description_view_3: Three flowers are visible, with the largest bloom facing forward and the others partially obscured, showing the layered petal arrangement.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged radially around a central disk, forming a starburst pattern with overlapping and slightly layered petals, exhibiting radial symmetry.
